About Me

I am broadly interested in using physics and computation to study chemical systems.

Currently, I am pursuing a double major in Biochemistry and Computer Science at the University of Maryland while doing research with Dr. Pratyush Tiwary.

For the past few semesters, I have been one of the two head teaching assistants for CMSC330, one of the largest computer science courses at my university with 500+ students each semester. I am also a teaching assistant for organic chemistry.

Outside of research and teaching, I used to speedsolve Rubik’s cubes, and I am an aspiring runner.
